Characters--and--NPCs/Alayna-Clorvus.title: Alayna Clorvus
Characters--and--NPCs/Alayna-Clorvus.content:
The impressionable young daughter of the Clorvus family. Her childish temperament and naiveté have resulted in quite a few perilous situations for those around her.

Lore
Description
A girl of around 12-14 years of age, Alayna has slightly curly blond hair with wide innocent hazel eyes. she is short of stature and often leaves her hair undone of any braid or bow. She wears two hairbands on her wrist out of habit. Her dress, although still obviously finery, is in a poor state of repair with the bottom of  the hem and a bit further up soiled.
Her voice is high pitched and a little sing-songy.
Characterization
The only child of Jonathan Clorvus and Jolene Clorvus, Alayna had a very easy childhood and she loved her parents. Spoiled with attention and gifts her whole life, she accepted and expected this as a base reality rather than a privilege many others didn’t or couldn’t receive. Nonetheless, she was not vindictive or ornery when not given what she thought to be her due but would rather take it as an attack on herself, as though she had done something wrong and was being punished.
Due to being highly sheltered by her parents, she also does not know many children her own age, much less has any friends her own age.
Alayna is also not particularly keen of mind. A bit airheaded, she is very easily convinced of things and highly impressionable, which makes it easy for her to ‘get her way’ as ‘her way’ is very often someone else’s way that they have simply explained to her as something she should desire. This does have its limits though, as try as her parents might they could not get her to be interested in her various lessons on how to be a proper young lady and learn at least the superficial political intricacies of Ramsted. Infact, she came to detest politics to a significant degree.
For all her faults, Alayna has a kind heart. She hates to see people hurting (unless convinced that the ‘hurt’ is something else ) and will generally try to do as she feels is right.
After a week of tutelage under Imponius, Alayna has gotten fluent in infernal cursing and acting like a thug from Avernus. Although she has actually never been there ever she will totally act as though she has and often talk about the opps she has smoked or the demons she kicks it with (she doesnt do anything of the sort).

A week of racism indoctrination will not cause this to stop, she will continue to sound like this except she will now also use ‘shifter’ or ‘shifta’ freely and make side comments about how wyrdkin are violent or savage or drop the classic cherry picked statlines like 13%-50%. she will still be earnest about it and not malicious, shes copying what shes been taught.

Other
In a fit of self-flagellating despair (and likely boredom), Alayna ran away from her home after BOTH her parents refused to sit and play with her again instead of going to another dull boring meeting about that horrid Church. This was the third time they refused her this month! Undoubtedly they hated her now.
Alayna found herself wandering through the streets, winding up in a strange part of town she had never been in before. Eventually, she found two lovely young men who finally asked her about what she was doing and why she was here. they claimed the Slums were not safe for her and that they would help her. Introducing themselves as The Brothers Tweed, they invited her back home where they explained their plans to join the Grand Church of Science to help with their problems at home. As Alayna had problems at home of her own (and out of the tiniest bit of rebellion) Alayna decided to go to the Church along with them.
A dizzying few weeks at Church left Alayna convinced that her parents along with everyone else in the city and likely a few people in the church itself as corrupt and in need of purification that only she could facilitate. Although, surely her oldest friends weren’t that bad right? this lapse in her ritual self isolation allowed the party to come in and eventually interrupt her, right when she was about to truly become one with science! she kicked and screamed as the giant barbarian pulled her from the the front of the massive metal double doors that were to slowly open and envelop her with the sight of the body of science…
As Alayna came to her senses, she sat in a room of a broken down house with her friends all around her, arguing amongst each other. they said they had some place to be and left her with a new friend who they said would take care of her. “Ugh, kids. Ah well I cant complain it could be a lot worse, I could be in literal hell” said the short odd looking man. ” You can call me Imponius - or actually I guess boss told me I’m Mr. Grumpy now. Whatever, don’t care. Now, you ever cast an eldritch blast before?”
After a week of intense linguistic expansion and intuited experiences gathered from much pantomime and charades, Alayna’s friends came back. they seemed quite a bit softer than they had previously, which struck her as a bit odd. Had that wrinkle skinned pansy always looked like something she could grind and smoke fouler than her opps souls getting caught pushing p in the 4? This time the party bought another person for her to befriend, but this fool looked straighter than the rod up Belials skinny ass, there was no way he could smoke as tough as she could or even hang with the spider demons on the byway of 1st and 1/3rd. The old geezer cleared his throat and bowed, his pompous accent almost manually rolling her eyes for her “Ehem, yes, madam clorvus jr! look at how big you’ve gotten! i haven’t seen you since you were - this tall! I am blessed to see you in such good health. we had heard terrible rumors and your mother was quite beside herself! very glad I came when I did. I apologize greatly for being unable to reunite you at once as I am sure you have been positively besides YOUR self at the horrid state of this place! not even any lace on these curtains!” The fat old man shivered, his jowls jiggling like a freshly blasted lemur. “Alas, you must take solace in the knowledge that you are not forgotten and we WILL collect you as soon as time permits! indeed, it may be safer for you here where the dirty shifters wont know to look”.

Goals
As a devout scientist and Sanctified Maiden, Alayna would stop at nothing to ensure the body of science be made whole.

after being mindwiped by Mr Scratch she is no longer a scientist or cares about them. possibly actively hostile

Alayna is now technically part of the party and does as she is told (provided someone is there looking after her)

Conflicts/Quests
Alayna is spying on the party secretly from afar.

Deacon Clein had told her others were not to be trusted, but her brief friendship with the party and The Brothers Tweed along with the brusque and abrupt commands from the deacon confuse her
The Party found her and discovered what she was doing as well as her impending duty

Alayna will be participating in the Sacrament of Science to untold effect.

the party stopped her from sacrificing herself to science

Alayna is not aware that her Father is dead…

or that technically 2 party members helped with that but the party members technically dont know that either yet so yknow
Alayna was made aware that her father was dead by the party when reuniting Alayna with Madam Clorvus. she seemed distraught.

Alayna is not aware that The Brothers Tweed are dead, killed by Bill in cold blood…
The Grand Church of Science is still looking for Alayna in the city
Alayna is being taught Wyrdkin racism by Rosenbaum Lent

Items

Locket with her parents pictures in it
Friendship bracelet (other one is owned by Imponius )

Characters--and--NPCs/Deacon-Clein.title: Deacon Clein
Characters--and--NPCs/Deacon-Clein.content:
one of the Deacons of the Grand Church of Science. Responsible for protecting the church from outside forces as well as furthering the churches interests over other groups in the city, he uses his contacts for personal gains as he is corrupt.

Lore
Description
A man of middling height and longer, greasy hair. His skin is sallow and pale while his eyes seem almost permanently narrowed. He keeps his mouth pressed into a hard line. His white cloak of deaconhood, while not perfectly clean at all times is nevertheless seemingly always wrinkle-free and pressed. His left eye has a slight twitch to it at times.
Characterization
Although he has a sizeable measure of power and was one of the strongest (arguably THE strongest figure) in Ramsted, Clein is a petty and childish man with a cruel streak. the quintessential bully, he enjoys punching down and panics if pressed or openly and directly confronted. He builds an outside persona of being cold and calculating while those close to him know him to be petulant and known to hold grudges. He is a sore loser and a bad sport.
While he may not be a smart as he would like to be believed, he IS conniving and has gotten fairly good at getting an edge out over people which is how he arrived at his position in the first place. Indeed, there are rumors of knives-in-the-dark surrounding Clein’s ascension to Deaconhood. For all his faults, Clein is still effective at his job.
Other
Cleins corruption is tacitly allowed/ignored by the Archbishop Willium Nylus as Clein does still provide the church with funding and keep some important operations running. However, this results in Deacon Dahc being at odds with Clein, seeing him for the corrupt weasel he truly is. The two posture and needle at each other constantly, with Deacon Dahc attempting to indict Clein in front of the Archbishop for at least one of his several crimes against the church.
Clein was responsible for the downfall of the City Government. originally pretending to be a reasonable man and mediating conflicts, he won the trust of the leaders of the government and began to turn them against each other. He also managed to get most of the city to be on the church’s side since the government had gone to them to see if they had any cure in the first place.
Recently, Clein has gotten more paranoid and anxious as he seems to believe that he may be being targeted by one of the several outgroups he has maintained contact with or colluded with.
Clein is also the deacon responsible for setting up the Tribulations as well as overseeing training for the Scalpels.
He seems to think he has a lot more control over the Scalpels (and particularly Nightshade Shadowsbane) than he may actually have.

Goals
Clein wishes to enrich himself and gain as much power as he can. The ulterior motives for this are to allow him to indulge his vices of gambling and women.

Conflicts/Quests
Clein has been using the Scalpels to carry out assassinations of various people around the city. He has been doing so for a variety of reasons, including not returning his romantic advances or trying to blackmail him regarding his gambling debts.

As the scalpels are generally bound to science, Clein justifies these killings by sending these victims a piece of the body of science and claims that the victim had stolen it. this justifies sending in scalpel(s) in retribution.
two of the more high profile assassinations of this style include  Jonathan Clorvus and Delmar Danth (although Danths death has more than one factor surrounding it).

Clein has been in talks with the Conclave of Lliira, ostensibly to draft limits and borders for where their influence would be tolerated in the city. In reality, Clein has been trying to see if he can get a share of the Bottled Joy revenue
Clein has previously attempted to get a better deal with The Orme House of Healing from their shipments of medicines to the church in order to line his own pockets. he attempted to blackmail the house by revealing to the rest of the Church that the House had dealing with the Conclave as well.

The House had countered by threatening to cut off the church’s medical supplies and innoculation ritual aids. Clein was in the process of trying to find an angle before he ran away.

Clein has had some dealings with the Wyrdkin, as he wanted to ensure safe passage through the sewers and to see if he could manipulate Immasi into becoming dependant on him.

this failed as Immasi refused to do either. thinking him to be a simple brigand with a ragtag bunch of refugees, clein had not attempted to deal with him just yet

He is being targeted by Madam Clorvus but doesnt know it. All remaining higher members of the City Government hate him and wish to destroy him
Clein was a regular at Ramsted Ranch and was very familiar with the ones who ran it as well as what they were doing behind the scenes.

Characters--and--NPCs/Delmar-Danth.title: Delmar Danth
Characters--and--NPCs/Delmar-Danth.content:
The intrepid adventurer, scientist, theologian, shipwright, sailor, writer, actor, musician, doctor, poet, architect, singer, songwriter, soldier, mercenary and detective to name his most recent exploits. Responsible for gathering The Party and investigating the Death Disease.

Lore
Description

physical description of the character

Characterization

explanation of who the character is in terms of their personality and some backstory

Other
Danth was staying at the Ramsted Tavern, and knew the bartender Tarlan Whisperwidth quite well.
Danth investigated much of the area and found out much about the ongoing conspiracies before he was murdered.

Goals
Danth’s other goals have seemingly been cut short. the only one that now continues is his final quest, to cure the Death Disease from the whole of the Ramsted area.

Conflicts/Quests
Danth’s body was found shriveled up AND surrounded by bits of green rock with his heart ripped out. This is a clue to the string of political murders carried out by Deacon Clein by using the Scalpels of Science.
Danth was investigating the creation of Bottled Joy and seeing how or why it managed to decrease the effectiveness of the Death Disease. This caused some degree of alarm in the Conclave of Lliira, who decided to see if they could recruit him into their money making scheme. They did so as they knew the Grand Church of Science had already soured with Danth and had branded him a heretic and attempted to run him out of town.
Danth had begun investigating the Grand Church of Science and uncovered several pieces of information regarding them. Aside from the general hierarchy of the Church, Danth had gotten to know Deacon Clein before his falling out with the Church.
Danth was told about the The Orme House of Healing and how they worked with the church in order to create their inoculation ritual
Danth did not have good insight with the Wyrdkin as he was a human.
Danth had knowledge of what was going on in The  Ranch, and while his notebook covers his disapproval, he does grimly note that these types of groups are all too common in the world. However, as he was not interested in the political state of the area, he did not dig too deep into the level of corruption involved. He talked with D Piddy about how the ranch and its people had avoided proportional targeting by the Death Disease but never recieved more than a coy invitation to find out.
Danth also had contact with the City Government, as they were in power when the Death Disease first took hold in the area. He had an ongoing correspondence with Jonathan Clorvus and Rosenbaum Lent regarding the history of the disease and its spread. however, the correspondence had stopped a few week before Danth’s death, as Clorvus indicated he feared for his safety for a while.

Danth’s Notes
On Science - #1
The Body of Science is a strange glowing rock. These people are worshipping something left before us by the great society thousands of years before us. There is no god associated with this or demon or any higher power. Perhaps the pope of these people is divine in some way?  The science is poisoning these poor fools and they dont even realise it. This does not appear to be part of the curse of Naymor. That has ancient evil associated with it. That must be elsewhere… I have seen things at night recently. I fear for my safety yet i cannot show it. I pray my friends appear soon. I have yet to find out more about this other than the legends and stories the townsfolk have, about showing romantic feelings publicly brings the curse upon you. All malarky and foolishness certainly. Who is kuyob? What connection might he have with this?? Too many questions, not enough leads. I will see what the conclave has to say to me tomorrow. Whatever deal they have better be worth it.

the doors talk and have distinct personalities

Characters--and--NPCs/Erwin-Earthsplitte.title: Erwin Earthsplitter
Characters--and--NPCs/Erwin-Earthsplitte.content:
The 11 year old mining foreman at the Hundred Hills Mine

Lore
Description
A young boy, youthful face always covered in the soot from torches and the dust and dirt of the earth in which he toils. He is extraordinarily strong for an 11 year old.
Characterization
Like Erwin from attack on titan, he is a charismatic and inspiring leader as well as a noble paladin of the earth and the ways of the mines. He never backs down and will always help those in need of aide especially if they are those sadly numerous weak and helpless creatures known as adults.
Other
Erwin is probably a lot older than 11 but there seems to be some magic involved that keeps him in a perpetual state of youth..

Goals
To stay in the mines and keep mining because the children yearn for the mines.

Conflicts/Quests

Clear out the Hundred Hills of the interlopers that would disrupt his noble task


Items
Transclude of Earthbreaker

Stats
Small humanoid (human), lawful good
Armor Class 17 (half plate armor)
Hit Points 95 (10d8 + 50)
Speed 25 ft., burrow 15 ft.

Ability Scores





















STRDEXCONINTWISCHA18 (+4)10 (+0)18 (+4)10 (+0)12 (+1)16 (+3)

Saving Throws
Str +9, Dex +4, Con +9, Wis +5, Cha +7
Skills
Athletics +13, Intimidation +7, Perception +5, Persuasion +7, Religion +4
Damage Resistances
Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing from nonmagical attacks
Condition Immunities
Frightened
Senses
Tremorsense 30 ft., Passive Perception 15
Languages Common, Dwarvish, Terran, Azer
Proficiency Bonus +4

Abilities
Divine Sense (4/long rest). Erwin can detect the presence of celestials, fiends, and undead within 60 feet until the end of his next turn.
Lay on Hands (50 HP pool). Erwin can restore hit points or cure diseases and poisons by touch.
Divine Smite. When Erwin hits with a melee weapon attack, he can expend a spell slot to deal additional radiant damage: 2d8 for a 1st-level slot, plus 1d8 for each spell level higher.
Fighting Style: Great Weapon Fighting. When Erwin rolls a 1 or 2 on a damage die for an attack with a melee weapon he’s wielding with two hands, he can reroll the die (must use new roll).
Extra Attack. Erwin makes two attacks when he takes the Attack action.
Aura of Courage (10 ft.). Erwin and friendly creatures within 10 feet can’t be frightened.
Divine Health. Erwin is immune to disease.
Stone’s Endurance (Recharge 5-6). When Erwin takes damage, he can use his reaction to reduce the damage by 1d12 + 5.
Earthen Fortitude. Erwin has resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from nonmagical attacks while standing on natural earth or stone.
Sacred Oath: Oath of the Miner. Erwin has sworn to honor the ancient calling of mining, believing it to be the holiest of all professions. He is bound by his tenets:

Dig Deep, Stand Tall: Never abandon a mine shaft or a companion
Strike True: Let each swing of the pickaxe be purposeful and just
Forge Forward: Never back down simply because the path forward is hard


Spellcasting
spellcasting ability is Charisma (spell save DC 15, +7 to hit with spell attacks). He has the following paladin spells prepared:
1st level (4 slots): Bless, Command, Divine Favor, Shield of Faith
2nd level (3 slots): Aid, Prayer of Healing, Lesser Restoration, Zone of Truth
3rd level (2 slots): Crusader’s Mantle, Meld into Stone, Spirit Shroud
Oath Spells (always prepared):

1st: Earth Tremor, Sanctuary
2nd: Spike Growth, Shatter
3rd: Erupting Earth, Meld into Stone


Actions
Multiattack. Erwin makes two attacks with Earthbreaker.
Vigor of Youth: twice per day, concentrate for 2 turns (need a DC 20 save for the concentration to be maintained). at the end of the 2 turns, bestow the benefits of a short rest on everyone within 25ft of him

Bonus Actions
Divine Favor (Self-cast, 1st level slot). Erwin’s attacks deal an extra 1d4 radiant damage for 1 minute (concentration).
Earthen Step. Erwin can burrow through nonmagical earth and stone as if it were difficult terrain, leaving no tunnel behind. He can use this movement on his turn without using an action.

Reactions
Stone’s Endurance (Recharge 5-6). Reduce incoming damage by 1d12 + 5.
Guardian’s Pickaxe. When a creature within 5 feet of Erwin is hit by an attack, Erwin can interpose Earthbreaker, granting the target +3 AC against that attack.

Characters--and--NPCs/FaiFil.title: FaiFil
Characters--and--NPCs/FaiFil.content:
A dimwitted yet still ferocious two headed ogre with an aspiration to climb the culinary heights

Lore
Description
Standing at a regular height and build for an ogre, Faifil would be lost in a group of them were it not for the extra head that jutted out at an angle from near the base of his thick neck. He holds a club that has seen much use in all manner of ways, from capturing things for consumption to flattening out grasses for a bed. He wears a loincloth and some
Characterization
The main head is slow in speech and thought but a bit more inclined to reason. the smaller head is fast in speech and thought and much more aggressive and instinctual. Neither is particularly intelligent and it is not too hard to trick them.
neither head really knows how to count.
Other
Faifil stopped terrorizing the local villages after being convinced by Silas that adults were much better meals because they were larger and could be cooked to enhance the flavors. He traded Faifil a mango chutney recipe in exchange for leaving in one piece with many promises of how great it was.

Goals
Faifil wishes to eat delicious tasty food and also to get full.

Conflicts/Quests
Faifil found a recipe for mango chutney and wishes to create it or try it as it described to be incredible by a previous meal.

The previous meal was Silas
Faifil captured a bard to test out this recipe
Mr Scratch tricked faifil into waiting for octopi to spawn by calling them mango chutney

Faifil has been terrorizing local area villages by capturing and eating the children there because he is hungry

will go back to this as he cant seem to get any more mango chutney and he would rather be full than hungry

Faifil is acquainted with Basogre who kicked Faifil out of the city and the sewers because Faifil was annoying any also couldn’t control himself and was attacking too many people which was drawing way too much attention to them.

doesn’t like Basogre because of this and would probably be down to hurt him

Characters--and--NPCs/Ferina.content:
A young woman from a small village on The Ramsted Coast. Brave of heart and resolute of will

Lore
Description
A woman in her early 20s, Ferina is short of stature with long hair that reaches her mid back. Her simple threadbare skirt and blouse reflect her upbringing in a fishing village without many luxuries to spare. Her slightly more defined muscles when compared to other women her age along with a tan denote a harder life where women must pitch in their share of manual labor as well.
After her transformation into an Awakened Mother, Ferina now has gills along the side of her neck, dark black scleras, slight webbing between her finger and toes, and 4 tentacles sprouting from her back (up to 8 can appear). her teeth also have become more sharp and her skin has become paler and tinged with blue. She also still has the symbol carved in the center of her chest still.
Characterization
Imaginative and free from a young age, Ferina had to become responsible from a young age due to the hardships in the village. In order to make money for their family, Ferina’s father would would head to Ramsted almost every day to sell fish and bycatch. The family became increasingly dependent on Ferina catching fish for them for her father to then go and sell a ways away at Ramsted. Nevertheless, she enjoyed life and loved her family, particularly her younger sister whom she spoiled and tried to provide as idyllic and carefree a life as possible. Indeed the faded pink ribbon her sister wears is a testament to Ferina’s nature, as she had given up the one bit of ornamentation she owned to bolster someone else’s happiness.
However, Ferina’s sacrificial nature does not mean she allows herself to be walked over, as people quickly found out. When pushed, she is firm and is prone to bouts of anger when particularly frustrated. For all her hardiness, Ferina is still sheltered and naïve in certain matters. she has not seen much of the wider world aside from a trip to Ramsted and is perhaps too trusting at times. She also loves the Sea.
Other
After she was captured, the sacrifice of her fellow villagers on top of her body to create a Linked Bond with the Sahaugin High Priestess had a lasting effect on her mental state. While initially overcome with grief and survivors guilt, this will eventually manifest as isolationism and cause her to withdraw from the village as she fears her powers as an Awakened Mother will only cause further problems for the ones she loves.. she will be hesitant to reach out to those willing to help her.

Goals
Prior to her kidnapping, Ferina’s main goal was to provide a good life for her family
Ferina’s main goal now is to keep her (now larger) family safe from harm by ousting The Astral Kraken from her waters

Conflicts/Quests
In a bid to save her sister from the sahaugins that attacked her in a raid on the village, Ferina distracted the monsters leading to her capture. She was taken into the Sahaugin Cavern, her fate unknown

Ferina was taken to a room with some other captured villagers and was subject to dark magics

The Sauhaugin High Priestess has taken an interest in Ferina and is using her as part of some dark ritual.

The ritual is to create an Awakened Mother.

Having become an awakened mother, the physical changes to her body cause her to panic, fearing retribution or ostracization from her family or the village

Mr Scratch fixed this by getting her to sign away her soul to his Patron, Mammon.
Joining the employ of Mammon will allow her to disguise herself yet it will cause her problems as she will not want to go out and do things for the company


With the sahaugin problems solved for the time being, The Astral Kraken remain at large. It continues to build its lair and found a foothold in the waters along The Ramsted Coast.

Characters--and--NPCs/Golomon-Sundry.title: Golomon Sundry
Characters--and--NPCs/Golomon-Sundry.content:
The mysterious undertaker of Ramsted. His origins are unknown as is what exactly he does with the bodies…

Lore
Description
A large man of prodigious girth, his clammy yellowed skin is covered with thin scars that criss cross his lumpy body. His skin is loose in places and tight in the other, almost seeming stitched together. His bald head is set with two small beady black eyes that occasionally betray a glimpse of a green corona within them.  his large flat mouth displays a smattering of teeth beset at odd angles within it. Golomon wears a tattered apron that might have once been white, and a pair of breeches in a similar state of slovenly neglect and disrepair.
Golomon pushes around a large two wheeled cart upon which he places the soon-to-be-interred. He keeps a large cleaver hanging from his belt and a odd large hook on his upper left arm, linked with a length of chain that wraps around his girth as a belt of sorts.
Characterization
Golomon is not particularly bright, but he is not a simpleton either. he delights in cruelty and is lazy and callous towards others. contrary to most with his unfortunate dental situation, his mouth seems perpetually curved in a downwards smile. He speaks in fits and bursts, his words and sentiments often off-putting and crude. He is also a bit of a shifty individual, cagey with questions and suspicious of those that ask too many.
Other

any lore or additional information that isn’t description

Characters--and--NPCs/Immasi.title: Immasi
Characters--and--NPCs/Immasi.content:
The strong-willed and keen leader of the Wyrdkin. While his past is shrouded in mystery, what is evident and obvious is that he will get what he perceives as justice for his people, through any means necessary.


“I will not rest till every drop of wyrdkin blood has been repaid with interest”

Lore
Description
A 6ft tall and leanly muscular WereRat, Immasi wears two longswords at his sides and a dagger at his back. Besides the cloth pants and leather sword belts that adorn his bottom half, the only armor he wears is a metal shoulder pauldron. Proud of his heritage, he walks around in his hybrid state of a bipedal, anthropomorphic rat.
Characterization
Immasi does not think very highly of human. In fact, he is outright dismissive and largely racist towards them. He is also holds a deep mistrust for them. However, this is likely in reaction to the centuries of abuse and oppression the Wyrdkin have undergone. Pushed into the Ramsted Sewers, he plots on how to exact his justice upon the hateful surface dwellers that have hurt his people.
He is shrewd and cunning, and knows that overthrowing the humans in control is not something that can be done overnight, which is why he has spent years preparing his people and gathering resources for this endeavor.
Other

Goals
Immasi’s main goal is to achieve control over Ramsted and the surrounding area, to create a safe haven for wyrdkin everywhere to be able to live.

Conflict/Quests
In order to gain Immasi’s trust, he requires shows of loyalty. After convincing him to not kill any non-shifters, he will ask for 4 things before revealing any details about himself or his plans.

To begin, Immasi will demand that the party search through the Ramsted Sewers to locate the Basogre’s Lair, and see if Basogre and his people could be convinced to join Immasi’s cause.

Following a successful handling or recruitment of Basogre, Immasi will deem the party useful, and will reveal the three tasks he needs completed before he will reveal his ultimate plan of Immasi’s uprising


Immasi will request that the party head to The Orme House of Healing to free the Wyrdkin trapped within the walls of the hospital, to be used as experiment fodder or ingredients.

Immasi has determined through rumors and investigation by one of his lieutenants Skem Shearpaws that the house of healing is manufacturing their effective medicines by utilizing the wyrdkins often magically imbued bodies/



Immasi wants the spread of Bottled Joy to be put to an end, as it is ravaging the ranks of the wyrdkin (who seem extra susceptible to it).

He knows that the Bottled Joy is peddled by theConclave of Lliira. and wants his people mentally and physically free for what is to come.



Immasi confesses that the Wyrdkin are not as well armed as he would like. This is due to the lack of metals coming in from Hundred Hills Mine. He would like for the flow of metals to commence again so that he can put to use some of his people who are capable blacksmiths to make custom weapons for the Wyrdkin.

His assaults on the Grand Church of Science’s weapon shipments have been getting thwarted repeatedly, which leads him to think that there is a wyrdkin mole in his midst. this has lead to Immasi getting more paranoid and harsh, possibly costing him some trust in followers that were already uneasy with his leadership as is.



Immasi wants to know what the other factions know about the wyrdkin and how ready they are for the coming assault. He would like to see what the party could discover

Immasi has excellent intelligence and already knows what the other factions (mostly just the Conclave of Lliira and the Grand Church of Science) know. he just wants to see how trustworthy the party is.



After all quests are done or ignored within the time frame (2 weeks in game time), Immasi will launch Immasi’s uprising in Ramsted, which will be a major point in the story/campaign with major consequences in how things operate in the city.
Immasi has a Patron Shard that can be collected after doing 3 of his quests or killing him
Immasi is explicitly at odds with the City Government, however he is not factoring them into his plans as he doesn’t think they are enough left to matter. he does know of Jonathan Clorvus, Rosenbaum Lent, Tom Thedrow, and Venka Arton but not in their newer ‘rebel’ or ‘outcast’ capacity. He also does not know much regarding Madam Clorvus or her organization efforts. This is largely because he doesn’t believe the racist and uppity humans would ever come down into the sewers which lets them get away with operating in the sewers under Immasi’s nose.
Immasi is getting money and food from Ramsted Ranch as he needs to keep his people fed somehow. They have so far only extracted promises of safety but Immasi knows that there are strings attached to this. The Ranch gives Immasi supplies in exchange for him kidnapping human(or humanoid, whatever) orphans for the ranch. Immasi has agreed to this on the condition that the wyrdkin children would not be touched. This agreement has been put in jeopardy by the Ranch revealing that they were the ones that hired the party all along.

Items

Characters--and--NPCs/Madam-Clorvus.title: Madam Clorvus
Characters--and--NPCs/Madam-Clorvus.content:
Jolene ‘Madam’ Clorvus is the current head of the ex-City Government. She took over after the death of her husband, Jonathan Clorvus. She is Alayna Clorvus’s mother.

Lore
Description
Once the envy of women and the object of several men’s desires, father time has begun to catch up with Madam Clorvus. While she is by no means an old crone, she cannot be described as youthful any longer. Her dark black hair has begun to silver and the lines have begun to appear on her visage, perhaps accelerated by the recent stress she has undergone. Her eyes remain as keen as ever though, noting the intellectually inclined mind behind them.
Characterization
Madam Clorvus was long content to enjoy the finer things in life, which her husband provided for her by virtue of his position in the government. She also enjoyed the occasional adrenaline pulsing romp of acting the innocent bimbo to spy for him. She preferred not to bother herself with the more mundane and likely boring minutia of actually governing the city and lifestyle she enjoyed. Perhaps if she had been more attentive in these matters she wouldn’t have found herself caught completely unaware by the acute downward spiral of the City Government at the hands of Deacon Clein. A testament to the ability of even her willfully unexercised intellect, she managed to piece together the truth of what was going on and stopped the four most powerful leaders of the government Tom Thedrow, Venka Arton, Rosenbaum Lent and her own husand Jonathan Clorvus from killing each other over Clein’s manipulations. However, it was too little too late and the illustrious and long held government of Ramsted had collapsed.
Other
After getting the goverment leaders back on the same page, her and her husband worked together to try to regain the trust of the people and undermine the church. In doing so, they perhaps neglected their young spoiled daughter. After the targeted assassination of her husband, she has fled into the Ramsted Sewers to protect herself and the fledgling city church resistance.
While she is no fan of Wyrdkin or particularly sympathetic to their cause, she is not racist against them and does think they should have been treated better.
She doesn’t actually know what goes on in Ramsted Ranch and thinks it is probably just some strip club and gambling joint. she doesnt want her leaders to be associated with the corruption and waste of the past and would be mad because of that (if she were to hear they were there recently).

Goals
Madam Clorvus would like to set up the City Goverment again, and also wants revenge against the church (and particularly deacon clein).
She also wants to find her daughter again and keep her safe as she is all that is left of her family

Conflicts/Quests
Estranged from Alayna Clorvus and wants to find and keep her safe. find Alayna and bring her back
Find out where Clein has gone and find a way to destroy him, preferably capture him
find a way to get the Conclave of Lliira out of the city and stop ruining the population with joy.
Undermine and remove the Grand Church of Science from ramsted

After repeated suggestions from Lent as well as information from Tom Thedrow, she thinks that Immasi’s uprising might be a perfect time to regain power and destabilize the church.

Characters--and--NPCs/Necrolord-Nyctasha.title: Necrolord Nyctasha
Characters--and--NPCs/Necrolord-Nyctasha.content:
A necromancer that has been quietly amassing immense power and influence behind the scenes. He contains a high amount of The Essence within him.
He is behind the spread of the Death Disease in the land by locating and imitating the Curse of Naymor and has a hand in or been a driving force behind almost every major event or conflict in the Ramsted area to further his cause (and the ritual).
Goals
Nyctasha desires power. As he is a necromancer, this leads to the logic conclusion of trying to trigger as much death as possible to expand his dominion. However, the Necrolord knows that having too much of The Essence within him would cause problems, and Divine Avatars of The Essence dissolve all too often. As such, he is doing what most being of sufficient knowledge of the workings of the universe do, which is to maintain a certain level of intrinsic power and surround yourself with powerful beings or creatures you can control.
The Curse of Naymor is part of this, where its use would allow him to supplant or consume the current strongest Essence Creature in the area, Mysterious Patron.
Once he has taken Mysterious Patron’s place, he will then send forth his armies across the land, killing and resurrecting people till he is the sole power on the planet.

Characters--and--NPCs/Nightshade-Shadows.title: Nightshade Shadowsbane
Characters--and--NPCs/Nightshade-Shadows.content:
The head Scalpel of the Grand Church of Science. Like his past, his face is shrouded in shadows. Due to his high levels of secrecy, there is a debate on if he is truly real or just a fanciful tale.

Lore
Description
Nightshade Shadowsbane stands at a middling height with an unassuming figure. He wields a wicked dagger and wears several throwing knives and other unknown weapons concealed over his body. He wears loose dark clothing with well made armor both under and on it. His eyes appear as twin glowing white lines floating in the unnerving silhouetted mass that is his head.
Characterization
While his past is truly unknown, the figure known as Shadowsbane does in fact exist. Terse in his manner of speaking and with an affinity for showing rather than telling, he is an efficient and ruthless machine who seems to do exactly as he is told.
However, it is clear to any who have interacted with him that he is no mindless minion content to do whatever his masters decide. While he has yet to disobey one, he questions orders and wishes to know the purpose behind things before he does them. He also takes to training new people/recruits with with what seems to be eagerness and a sort of pleasure.
Other

Characters--and--NPCs/Puss.title: Puss
Characters--and--NPCs/Puss.content:
A sly Tabaxi rogue with a penchant for dramatic flair, not much is known about his origins.

Lore
Description
A large black hat, grown slightly floppy around the brim with just a tinge of patina on the velvet shows the age and adventures this hat and the particular adventurer it belongs to have undergone. Its wide expanse shades the sharp emerald eyes of the wily, orange furred tabaxi that wears it. Clad in swashbuckling boots and loose baggy trousers with a feather in his hat and a vest on his back this character oozes the self confident charm of a professional snake oil salesman.
Characterization
Puss loves gambling, it is a vice that can often get the better of him. He is a cheat at cards though. While he has no interest in doing heroics and will readily rob people, he does have a sense of morality which will cause him to scam the less fortunate to a lesser degree.
Puss will also very easily let others sacrifice themselves for him.
He is a free spirit, not caring for Immasi‘s call to action to all Wyrdkin and thinks that the idea of Immasi’s uprising would be bad for business. Of course, his business is scamming and stealing from people but who’s counting ?
Other
He is the leader of Puss’s Crew. Generally not interested in religion, he is superstitious about Lady Luck in hopes of getting better gambling wins.

Goals
Puss and his crew are essentially another DnD party, but these guys are sort of running around trying to get as rich as possible. They will be interested in magic items.
find and free his brand new castle from the basilisk scourge (oneshot? quick side campaign?)

This will likely be a part of the kuyob stuff when party gets there eventually


Conflicts/Quests
Puss and his crew are avoiding directly working for Immasi and would prefer to not be part of Immasi’s uprising as it would make life harder for them because there would be less stuff to steal
Puss and his crew are at Ramsted Ranch trying to case the place and find out where the gold is actually kept

Acrobatics +13, Athletics +4, Deception +7, Intimidation +7, Perception +6, Performance +7, Sleight of Hand +9, Stealth +13
Senses
Darkvision 60 ft., Passive Perception 16
Languages Common, Thieves’ Cant, Elvish, Animals
Proficiency Bonus
+4

Features
Feline Agility. When Puss moves on his turn in combat, he can double his speed until the end of the turn. Once he uses this trait, he can’t use it again until he moves 0 feet on one of his turns.
Cat’s Claws. Puss has retractable claws that deal 1d4 + 5 slashing damage. These are natural weapons.
Cat’s Talent. Puss has proficiency in Perception and Stealth.
Cunning Action (Rogue). Puss can take a bonus action to Dash, Disengage, or Hide.
Sneak Attack (5d6). Once per turn, Puss deals an extra 5d6 damage when he hits a target with a weapon attack and has advantage on the attack roll, or when the target is within 5 feet of an ally that isn’t incapacitated and Puss doesn’t have disadvantage.
Uncanny Dodge. When an attacker Puss can see hits him with an attack, he can use his reaction to halve the attack’s damage.
Evasion. When Puss is subjected to an effect that allows him to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, he instead takes no damage on a success and half damage on a failure.
Action Surge (1/short rest).
Second Wind (1/short rest). 1d10 + 10 hit points.
Fighting Style: Dueling. When wielding a melee weapon in one hand and no other weapons, Puss gains +2 to damage rolls (already included).
Extra Attack. Puss can attack twice, instead of once, whenever he takes the Attack action on his turn.

Actions
Multiattack. Puss makes two attacks with his rapier.
Rapier. Melee Weapon Attack: +9 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 11 (1d8 + 7) piercing damage.
Dagger (Thrown). Ranged Weapon Attack: +9 to hit, range 20/60 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (1d4 + 5) piercing damage.
Kitty Eyes (3/day). Puss locks eyes with one creature within 30 feet. The target must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw or be Charmed until the end of his next turn. If the target fails by 5 or more, Puss can choose what they do for their next action.

Bonus Actions
Cunning Action. Dash, Disengage, or Hide.
Second Wind (1/short rest). Regain 1d10 + 10 hit points.

Reactions
Uncanny Dodge. Halve the damage of an attack.
Riposte (When available). When a creature misses Puss with a melee attack, he can use his reaction to make one melee weapon attack against that creature.

Characters--and--NPCs/Rosenbaum-Lent.title: Rosenbaum Lent
Characters--and--NPCs/Rosenbaum-Lent.content:
On of the leaders of the Ex-City Government, he was tricked into almost killing his fellow associates by Deacon Clein. He is racist towards the Wyrdkin even by government standards .

Lore
Description
A relatively portly man, rosenbaum is getting on in his years. His hair and beard along with his large puffy mustache have all begun to grey and whiten, with his hair all but gone and pushed over in a rather sad looking combover. this whole affair generally remains hidden under a hat, usually a small bowler or modest tophat. His face reddens easily and he is not very athletic.
Generally well dressed in fashions that are slightly outdated, he wears full leather armor with a more substantial breastplate when he dresses up for his conclave job.
Characterization
Lent is self important and pompous. He is also not particularly intelligent and can be covetous and easily duped. He is not easily trusting as much as he is easily flattered, which allows for it to be easy to get one up on him. He can also be made to believe most things that would feed into his long held prejudices. Lent is racist towards Wyrdkin, not like angry racist but looks down and thinks of them as lesser racist. he does not think that wyrdkin can be capable of anything of note and mostly blames them for the death disease and also thinks they probably helped bring down the city government.
Other
Lent has no powers beyond those any disgraced politician may have of deflecting questions and shirking responsibility. To help account for this, he has an array of magical items that help him pose as a cleric.
He knows of immasi, mostly as a rabble-rousing firebrand that the city had dealt with back in the day. He truly thinks of the wyrdkins as lesser, so imagines that a firm stance and some corporal punishment will bring them into line. as a result of this, he views Immasi’s uprising as more of a chance to restore government control

Goals
Lent wishes to restore proper human control to Ramsted.

Conflicts/Quests
Lent is the City Government’s primary agent in the Conclave of Lliira and advises Madam Clorvus on what to do regarding the conclave.

He will reveal their currently unstable status to clorvus, which will embolden clorvus

Lent will champion getting the city government involved in using the uprising to restore government power to the city

Lent doesnt have the greatest of relationships with Tom Thedrow but he supports the plan to use the uprising to benefit the church.
Both oppose Venka Arton’s plan of escaping the city

Lent would prefer to see the wyrdkin flushed out of the city entirely or kept in the sewers

Items

Staff of Healing
Ring of regneration
Scrolls of cure wounds
potions of health
ring of health

Characters--and--NPCs/Tarlan-Whisperwidt.title: Tarlan Whisperwidth
Characters--and--NPCs/Tarlan-Whisperwidt.content:
The owner and proprietor of The Blustery Boot.

Lore
Description
Standing at 9 feet tall and broad of frame, Tarlan strikes an imposing figure. While mostly humanoid, his large horns and broad nose complete with a ring give away his Wyrdkin status. His perpetually rolled up sleeves show off his muscular forearms which many could swear have a cup and rag at the ends rather than hands. He wears a clean white shirt that has gotten yellower with age and simple rough spun brown pants.
Characterization
Very gruff and a man of few words when he speaks. His voice is deep. mostly snorts unless pushed very hard. He prefers hard honest work rather than a life of many luxuries. While he was much more wild and spirited in his youth, he is a lot more reserved and of the ‘protect your own’ mindset in his current age. while he is not hard hearted, he truly believes that everyone earns their karma and does not weep for friends that die in service of what they desire.
Other
An ex-adventurer, he is one of Delmar Danth’s many connections and could have been called up by him for a quest if things had gone differently.
He is a strong druid and bard

Keep the tavern safe from problems

Characters--and--NPCs/The-Astral-Kraken.title: The Astral Kraken
Characters--and--NPCs/The-Astral-Kraken.content:
An intelligent and malevolent creature from beyond, whose origins are shrouded in mystery.


“The skies wept and wailed while the very sea itself seemed to tremble at the sight of the horizon being torn asunder…and from that gash in reality appeared my purpose and my nightmares.”

From the log of Dread Captain Bill


Lore
Description
The species usually lives in The Astral Plane, of the many Planes of Existence. In The Mortal Plane it usually resides in deep space.
Characterization
Other
Goals
It has tried to create itself a lair, and after an unsuccessful attempt to wrestle control of a sahaugin settlement due to the creation of an Awakened Mother,
Known Abilities
It created and used an Avatar to exercise its will, something it subconsciously knew how to do due to the concentration of The Essence it has within it.

Characters--and--NPCs/The-Brothers-Tweed.title: The Brothers Tweed
Characters--and--NPCs/The-Brothers-Tweed.content:
Known to their friends and family as Eldy and Eldum, these young men are naïve and adventurous and wish to see the world while also helping their family.

Lore
Description
Both standing at a medium height for their 16 years of age, their black hair and earnest brown eyes made them seem younger than they were.
Characterization
Fraternal twins, Eldy and Eldum were inseparable from birth. Born as the eldest in a family the eventually came to have 4 younger siblings, they were forced to become the breadwinners of the family after their fathers passing to the death disease and the more mundane yet equally crippling sickness of their mother.
While they knew they had to help their family, they also hungered for adventure. They knew of only 1 possible way to satisfy both needs, which was to join the Grand Church of Science and attain a good enough rank to be sent out to expeditions. That way, they could earn a living for their family as well as seeing what the world had to offer.
Other
The brothers met Alayna Clorvus in the streets of the Ramsted Slums. They befriended her and while they knew there was more to her story as someone like her and the clothes she wore were not common in the slums, they offered companionship without any invading questions. Eventually, they all decided to make for the church, for their own personal reasons.
Following their achievement of the rank of Seeker, the boys joined with Bill, Edon, Thaw’aa, and Yobi to go on their first quest for science. As they had never left the city prior to this, they were incredibly enamored by Edon and the fantastical tales he told of his mercenary adventures throughout the land.
Tragedy struck in the fight at the kobold cave as Eldy was overwhelmed in the fighting. Bill, having suffered enough of the children’s ramblings this trip decided to do Eldy a mercy and kill him to send him to his brother…

Goals
Eldy and Eldum want to find treasure and glory out in the world so they may be able to help their family.

Conflicts/Quests
Make it back to ramsted with money for the tweed family

obviously failed lol

Characters--and--NPCs/The-Essence.title: The Essence
Characters--and--NPCs/The-Essence.content:
One of the Foundation Deities. It, and its substance, are the core of all magics in the multiverse. However, it requires creation to and cultivation to truly exist and it is just as often half sentient as it is sentient. This is due in part to the speed at which it will gather its influence and concentrate its power into beings. It is also capable of concentrating its power into several things across worlds at once, thought to be a side effect of the fact that basically every artifact or concentration of magic is actually just a concentration of The Essence.
It is hungry yet possessive of what was made from it, which is basically most things. Creatures with high concentration of The Essence will often lean more and more into baser instincts and have incredible power.
Goals
It is as of yet unclear if The Essence has any long term goals

Characters--and--NPCs/The-Wrath.title: The Wrath
Characters--and--NPCs/The-Wrath.content:
An Ancient Tarrasque. Local tradition holds that the lands around Ramsted were terraformed or created by The Wrath‘s terrible power.

The Wrath is at the center of a few cults and mystic traditions. It is also sometimes known as Nilbog the Mad. It is a popular choice for local death cults in need of something to revere.
Its calcified body gave rise to The Hundred Hills.
The Lady of Sorrows attempted to revive The Wrath with the help of a death cult and a strange arcane construct (which yields a max of 3 Kuyobian Keys if disassembled correctly)
The Wrath may have been an avatar of The Essence in its time…

Characters--and--NPCs/Tom-Thedrow.title: Tom Thedrow
Characters--and--NPCs/Tom-Thedrow.content:
Ex City Government member assigned to the Grand Church of Science to keep tabs on them and learn more about their workings.

Lore
Description
A drow with a rough stubble, bristly mustache and a squarish face. His ears are fat and only have the barest point up at the peak, leading for him to be often mistaken for a darkskinned human. A rough edged scar goes down over his left eye, and his ridged posture makes him look like one you would rather not cross.
Characterization
Has the exaggertaed british accent, oi mate u avin a jerk mate
Aggressive and prone to action rather than lofty talk, Tom is also suspicious and not very quick to trust. An accomplished fighter from his childhood spent on the streets, he prefers to get in peoples faces rather than threaten to do so.
However, he is prone to flattery and can be swayed by buttering him up enough.
Other
Tom is an accomplished spy and fairly good at it, all things considered. he was stationed in the church for a while and has gathered intelligence for the government on the things the church has been up to. He has leaked that the church is also looking for Deacon Clein and that Deacon Dahc is currently in over his head trying to keep the church in order. He did not realize alayna was at the church as during important ceremonies and things he was sneaking off to the deacons offices looking for information.
After Clorvus, he was Delmar Danth’s best friend in the government. Not super bent up over danths death as he isnt much scared of death.

Goals
Wishes to restore the City Government to their role. Wants to run the church out of town

Conflicts/Quests
Will recognize the party from the church. will also recognize that they work for the deacons. not sure if he knows they work for clein or not.
doesnt know where alayna was being hidden because he was too interested trying to figure out what the deacons+archbishop were up to.
Would like to sabotage the church weapons. will need help with this.
possibly sabotage the church building itself.

Cloven-Council.title: Cloven Council
Cloven-Council.content:
The Druidic Circle of The Twin Wood.

Lore
Characterization
The Cloven Council is set in very traditional druidic ways, beholden to a creed of nonintervention or conflict, focusing on saving animals and the forest in as many ways as they can without going out and contacting non druids and such. Their main charge is to ensure the safety of the Heart of the Forest (even though they do not actually know where it is or anything).
They are suspicious of new ways of doing things.
They are intellectuals and are into the scientific process and stuff even though they are all magic users and stuff. will talk about publishing papers and such (publishing a paper is getting the paper encircled into the main tree of the grove, basically an inscription into the bark)

Ercima-Centira/Body-of-Science.title: Body of Science
Ercima-Centira/Body-of-Science.content:
The ‘Body of Science’ are radioactive material that are left over from the nuclear war that wiped out all life 10000 years ago. the church looks for these pieces and tries to find out which of the material is actually reactive uranium and what is waste.
in story it is anything that is green and kinda glows, the rituals of the church are in large part to figure out what is and isnt trash

Ercima-Centira/Divine-Avatar.title: Divine Avatar
Ercima-Centira/Divine-Avatar.content:
A being with highly concentrated amounts of a Foundation Deities power.
These beings are the how these deities interact with the multiverse across Planes of Existence.
Divine Avatars can bestow aspects of their divinity into other creatures. Gathering enough of a Foundation Deities power into a mortal will eventually override the mortals owns desires and personality and will convert it into a divine avatar.
Getting two Divine Avatars in close proximity will cause them to meld together(often a cataclysmic event) and if the total amount of divinity is over the set cap, the Deity will be banished for a while and held in contempt of the Influence Pact.

Ercima-Centira/Ercima-Centira.title: Ercima Centira
Ercima-Centira/Ercima-Centira.content: The First World created in Ameel’s Homebrew DnD setting. While there are supposed to be multiple worlds, and even multiple dimensions, nothing is fleshed out to the level of Setax and more specifically, Ramsted and the surrounding areas which are in Western Setax . There is only one land mass in Ercima Centira but this has different ‘continents’ within it. The name is simply an anagram for America Center, as the lands are all supposed to be different parts of a post apocalyptic America, ravaged by a massive nuclear war 10,000 years ago. The war is known to the few who know about it (such as Grand Church of Science and Divine Avatars) as The First Descent of Science .

Ercima-Centira/Foundation-Deities.title: Foundation Deities
Ercima-Centira/Foundation-Deities.content:
Being of Immense and Unimaginable power that were responsible for creating the multiverse. The three beings widely regarded as the most important or perhaps the only Foundation Deities are The Rodfather , The Potion Master, and The Essence. Of these, the two that have direct impact on the world are The Rodfather and The Potionmaster, with The Essence being more of a formless mostly sentient mass of magic, similar to Arc Warden. Both of the other two deities are technically at the mercy of the essence, as it is what allows them to create however it also relies on the two to be able to maintain a consciousness. The rods and potions balance each other by requiring each other (a bottle is simply an open top rod, while a rod needs a potion(lava or liquid glass etc) to be made.)
Also known in other worlds or parts of the planet as the trichotomy, the triumvirate, the three, the two, the creator and the corruptor, etc

Ercima-Centira/Infernal-Backdoor.title: Infernal Backdoor
Ercima-Centira/Infernal-Backdoor.content:
A rift between the infernal plane and others, made via illicit methods and usually undocumented

Lore
Description
A jagged, warped slash in the air. the edges of the suspended portal curl inward slightly, as though it were the burned edges of a paper. The image of the plane beyond waft and waves with an invisible heat.
Other
While temporary portal between dimensions can be opened by any mage of sufficient power, the lords of Hell do not care to police them, partly due to the flighty nature of mages of that stature and other magical protections to limit any influence they may have.
However, An infernal backdoor is a more long term entrance, once that can be used multiple times after its creation. These are a primary cause of concern for the Archdemons, as significant parts of their power and wealth come from taxing1 the entrances to their domains.  As such, the lords of hell spend a fair but on border security to make sure all entrances are documented.

Conflicts/Quests
Jorder Bopper mans the operations of 5 Infernal Backdoors that are present at Ramsted Ranch
All the lords of hell are quite keen on not having their border security infringed upon

Ercima-Centira/Influence-Pact.title: Influence Pact
Ercima-Centira/Influence-Pact.content: The oldest pact ever made, between the three foundational deities as a means of maintaining the fabric of the multiverse.

Ercima-Centira/Kissed-by-Science.title: Kissed by Science
Ercima-Centira/Kissed-by-Science.content:
A phenomena of note in the Grand Church of Science. Affecting only the Sanctified Maidens, this blessing manifests upon the Maiden as blistered patches of skin (where science kissed them), a shedding of hair (as science plays with it), weakness (breathless at the sight of science), nausea (being forced away from science), weight loss (the body becoming more beautiful for science), shaking (from excitement at the thought of going back to science), and bloody bowel movements (the body removing impurities before it goes to science again).
The opposite to this is to be spurned by science, which is either an instant death or burns upon the body.

Ercima-Centira/Kuyob's-Menagerie.title: Kuyob's Menagerie
Ercima-Centira/Kuyob's-Menagerie.content: First step to getting to Kuyob’s Throneroom. it involves getting through several complex puzzle rooms all of which are magically fantastical in some way or another

Ercima-Centira/Kuyob's-Pocket-Dimension.title: Kuyob's Pocket Dimension
Ercima-Centira/Kuyob's-Pocket-Dimension.content: A pocket dimension that exists in a space outside the universe in the multiverse somewhere. It will mimic the original surroundings of whomever entered it. It contains the Tower of Kuyob and will also have whichever of the Doors of Kuyob were used to enter it. this allows The Grand Wizard Kuyob to better guess where he is at the moment.

Ercima-Centira/Organizations/Conclave-of.title: Conclave of Lliira
Ercima-Centira/Organizations/Conclave-of.content:
The followers of Lliira, Goddess of Joy. This particular sect follows the teachings of the great sage Farzameel and his central idea that true joy can only be achieved from the dota prayer

Lore
Description
The cult formed around the worshipping of Lliira. Found in pockets all around the world, the Lliirans have developed a reputation for being good natured and helpful, as their god loves joy and the joyful. Conclaves of Lliira therefore are often known to go to areas stuck by tragedy or hardship to help out and bring joy to people as their form of worship.
Other

Goals
The original goal of the Conclave as a whole is to recover all the steps of the dota prayer to unlock the secret of eternal joy
The goal of this branch of the conclave’s leadership Lavve Genba is to get filthy rich

Conflicts/Quests
Even though they have connection with Deacon Clein, the conclave would rather the influence of The Grand Church of Science would diminish in the city to give full control to the conclave.
The Conclave is in uproar over the attack on the hospital. The followers or Lliira want to go help while the leaders are incredulous that their supplier was hit. Either way, there needs to be something done about the increasingly agitated number of junkies getting closer to the Conclave Building.
The leaders of the Conclave would like psychic protection robes and other articles
the conclave wants to knows that the only institution that could have hurt their supply chain (ruined latest shipments of potion) is Ramsted Ranch and will have sent a leader to demands answers from them

Locations
Lliiran conclaves are found all over the world wherever there might be pain or suffering, as a way to bring joy back to people.
see Conclave Building for the physical layout and people within the ramsted branch.

Ercima-Centira/Organizations/Grand-Churc.filePath: Ercima Centira/Organizations/Grand Church of Science Hierarchy.md
Ercima-Centira/Organizations/Grand-Churc.title: Grand Church of Science Hierarchy
Ercima-Centira/Organizations/Grand-Churc.content:
See Grand Church of Science
Pope of the Grand Church of Science
The head of the church, he oversees everything about the church. A long and storied position, the nature and personality of the pope has widespread ramifications on its actions and operations

High Clergy
Cardinal of the Grand Church of Science
A group of the most important members of the church. The pope’s successor is chosen from amongst the cardinals by the previous pope. This leads to many cardinals curry for favor with the pope rather than doing their administrative duties over their designated regions.
Cardinals can be established in the Central Grand Church or in any particular church within their region.
Archbishop of the Grand Church of Science
Leaders of established churches. They can only be superseded if they are in the same church as a Cardinal. They are responsible for overseeing the churches efforts in a smaller area than the cardinals and are supposed to work to achieve their cardinals vision, which in turn matches the overall efforts of the church. However, archbishops can sometimes be overly ambitious as they can be elevated to Cardinal by the pope.
The Archbishop is often the highest position of the church that an average scientist could have the chance to meet. They are rarely seen outside of particular religious events where they are officiating. A direct command from the Archbishop is law. While the two positions higher can obviously override an archbishops command, this will basically never happen as the higher positions would never stoop down to the minutia of giving orders or tasks to the church class.

Special Class
Sanctified Maiden of the Grand Church of Science
A part of the Special Class of scientist that is outside the standard hierarchy of the Grand Church of Science. They are of critical importance as the lynchpin in one of the holiest rituals of the Church, the Sacrament of Science. The Sanctified Maidens enjoy a position of reverence that could eventually even equal to the Archbishop. However, this does not mean that they have the same amount of power. Indeed, Sanctified Maidens are usually managed by the Deacon who will specifically pick out potential maidens during recruitment and ensure they are given the requisite attention and information the holy rank requires.
A Sanctified Maiden’s level of reverence depends on how their holy meeting with Science went. If they were accepted and Kissed by Science then they are highly revered for the remainder of their tenure on the mortal coil before going to join science. However, if they were instead spurned by science, it is a great shame and they are of less note than a Deacon (yet still above the Worker class and the other church classes.)
Scalpels of the Grand Church of Science
Highly trained and specialized agents of science. Training of these operatives is the true reason for the Tribulations. Masters of espionage and  assassination, this secretive order is due no small amount of credit for the Church’s reputation for quickly ingraining and asserting itself into towns. They are at the direct call of the Deacons, although their orders could be superseded by anyone with more power than one. Their authority can never be lower than a Deacons, but could be higher depending on who assigned them to a particular task. They are the secret pride of the church yet their names are whispered by the lower classes with a healthy dose of fear. It is rumored that the best of the Scalpels are in the direct employ of the Pope…

Church Class
Deacon of the Grand Church of Science
A highly important and respected position amongst the Church. The Deacons are responsible for the daily happenings of the church and the direct problems that arise . They are responsible for the designing and overseeing of the Tribulations as well as allocating resources and organizing quests to recover pieces of science. There are usually at least 2 deacons per parish of science. This is the highest position a scientist may expect to see regularly.
While it is not always the case, many Parishes have a Deacon responsible for the religious duties and another responsible for the political struggles the church must undergo.
Instructors of the Grand Church of Science
A very important position that is responsible for the training of new acolyte scientists as well as ensuring logistics for science retrieval quests. They are also in charge of daily clerical duties and specific tasks around their parish building (such as head librarian, head of conversions, etc.). This role is almost always comprised of more experienced convertors who have retired from the field after completing their area missions, but on occasion acolytes are groomed for the position.
Enforcers of the Grand Church of Science
Another very important position, Enforcers are responsible for the safety of the church and those within it. They are mostly made up of Searchers who have successfully completed a set number of science retrieval quests. Their martial prowess is passed onto acolytes and the worker class through trainings for retrieval quests. Aside from guard duties they also fill important roles such as Quartermaster, Head Armorer, etc.

Worker Class
Searchers of the Grand Church of Science
Part of the core classes that allow the church to function. Searchers are skilled individuals identified in the Tribulations to be good candidates for retrieval of pieces of the body of science from known or highly likely locations. It is the most coveted of the Worker positions by most acolytes.
Convertors of the Grand Church of Science
Equal in importance yet less coveted by the masses, the Convertors of the Grand Church of Science Engage in theological warfare with opposing religions in the area or simply go out to bring potential scientists to the fold. If successful, the largely cushy and important promotion of Instructor is enticing. Convertors can go on Science Retrieval missions if they volunteer.
Seekers of the Grand Church of Science
Least desirable and often ridiculed, to be a Seeker indicated poor performance in the Tribulations. Largely set to guarding Convertors, Seekers head out into the world to find pieces of the body of science. Given the lack of any resources allocated to this, they are usually unsuccessful. However, discovering a true piece of the body of science could promote their station to Searcher although this is a very rare occurrence, if it ever has truly happened.
Acolytes of the Grand Church of Science
Fresh recruits to the church who have yet to prove themselves in the tribulations, Acolytes are given grunt work of cleaning or organizing things interspersed with lessons of the church and activities to improve their skills for potential quests.

Blasphemers
Unbelievers
Those that do not believe in Science. Some are simply lost and it is the duty of the Convertors to guide them into the fold. They are not respected but can be conversed with.
Other-Believers
Those that believe in one of the heretical false gods. they were the ones who bought about the calamity of the The First Descent of Science and are the worst of humanity. They must change their ways or be eradicated. Derided, ridiculed, lambasted and damned.

Ercima-Centira/Organizations/Grand-Churc.title: Grand Church of Science
Ercima-Centira/Organizations/Grand-Churc.content:
Followers of the First Prophet Xarrod, who preached about the evils of false unsubstantiated faiths and the one true god of the world, science

Lore
The Grand Church of Science was formed 9000 years ago, when the first prophet began to explain to the people of Ercima Centira what had occurred when the world changed in the new era. He explained that due to people worshipping all sorts of false treasonous gods, Science descended upon the land with a fury, cleansing the world of the unbelievers. Now Science has gone to slumber and requires his followers to bring together his body. once the Body of Science has been reconstructed, Science will finally descend upon the land again and bring salvation to his people and fury upon the evil.
Leadership of the Church is noted in Grand Church of Science Hierarchy
Goals
To reconstruct the body of science there must be quests sent out to to track it and bring it back to the church. There are suspected areas of sciences resting places around the Ramsted area which are to be investigated and processed.
Deacon Clein is trying to expand his personal wealth and general influence by abusing church institutions to his own gain. he has his fingers in several other groups, all of which seem to be suspicious of him.
Deacon Dahc is trying to pin down Deacon Clein for his several crimes including those against the church (such as embezzlement or being a possible traitor ). More theologically involved, he also seeks to further church’s goals above all else.
Quests/Conflicts
The church seeks to be the sole power in a city so that it can use all of its resources to carry out quests to find the body of science. to this end, they wish to destroy all other factions of apparent power. In Ramsted, this would be the Conclave of Lliira and the City Government

Deacon Clein succeeded in the latter, with the government being a splintered shell of what it once was

Obtain parts of the body of science and combine it with the larger Body of Science under the church.

to this end, the Church may need a new (perhaps a few) Sanctified Maiden.

Discover who or what has been directing attacks against the church, primarily in regards to the Lustcubus.

Deacon Dahc believes this to be the work of Clein, in league with outside forces
Church rumors also point fingers at Ramsted Ranch, as they are known to be ‘demon worshippers’

The Church is now also intent on protecting the remaining 3 Relics in the library. they also want to know who has taken the relics so they may be brought to justice.

Chief amongst the suspects is the unknown and strangely named John Lennon, who is wanted for impersonating an agent of a Deacon.

Locations
See Ramsted GCS Parish for information about the physical building and what is present inside it

Ercima-Centira/Organizations/House-of-He.title: House of Healing Hierarchy
Ercima-Centira/Organizations/House-of-He.content:
General Hierarchy
Doctors
The heads of the hospital. each of the 4 are responsible for a different aspect of the house and mostly stay out of the others ways. They are the highest authority in the hospital, even if they like to vaguely talk about a ‘board of directors’ when convenient.
Head Nurse
The right hand man/woman of their respective doctor. The position is one that doubles as a personal secretary often, but more involved in the day to day activities of their particular wing as well as anything the doctor requires
Nurse
Technically on the same level of authority as the Head Nurse, the regular nurses will often defer to what the Head Nurse says. Nurses run the House and are responsible for everything from stabilizing emergency patients to cleaning out bed pans.
Receptionists
One for each wing of the hospital, these short odd fellows all look the exact same yet have vastly varying personalities. Employees of the House, they are lowest in the chain of command but are still vested some imagined authority over patients by virtue of their position. Their odd behaviors further a sense of mystery regarding them. The current receptionists are Sarjiv, Barjiv, Narjiv, and Carjiv.

Role Specifics
The General Doctor
The role with the highest turnover of the 4 Doctors of the House. It is the most isolated from the other roles, and is the face of the House. While well paid, there never seems to be a doctor that lasts here for more than a couple of years
The Doctor of Finance and Fundraising
The position currently held by Ave Rishus. This position is meant to secure funding for the hospital as well as ensure facilities are kept up to date. All monetary decisions require the Doctors final approval before they are put into effect.
The Doctor of Disease and Viruses
The position once held by Ivebinr Aisendead. This position is meant to cure and find cures to diseases as well as figure out ways to prevent them. It also is responsible for the House morgue and doing medical autopsies
The Doctor of Research and Experimental Medicine
The position held by Dolor Inflictus. This position is meant to find knew medical treatments for all sorts of problems as well as develop new and more effective solutions to previously used treatments. the position is also intended to make new medicines.

Ercima-Centira/Organizations/The-Izubar-.title: The Izubar Counting House
Ercima-Centira/Organizations/The-Izubar-.content:
The oldest and most respected financial institution in Setax (and possibly the world, will have to see later), its money is always good and will be lent on time for reasonable rates.

They cultivate this aura of trust by inculcating the fact of a heartless and unrelenting ability to make good on their investments by obtaining collateral - no matter what.

“The counting house will lend you money for a better rate perhaps…but keep in mind that the worst I could do is kill you..”                                              - popular saying amongst local money lenders

Ercima-Centira/Sacrament-of-Science.title: Sacrament of Science
Ercima-Centira/Sacrament-of-Science.content: One of the Holiest rituals in the Grand Church of Science, it is the process by which the discovered pieces of the Body of Science is returned to science to help make him whole again.

Ercima-Centira/Setax/Curse-of-Naymor.title: Curse of Naymor
Ercima-Centira/Setax/Curse-of-Naymor.content:
The curse of naymor is a legend as well as a powerful necromantic ritual.
The Death Disease is a side effect of the Curse of Naymor activating.

Ercima-Centira/Setax/Immasi's-uprising.title: Immasi's uprising
Ercima-Centira/Setax/Immasi's-uprising.content:
Immasi’s grand plan that he has been working towards. After gathering a sizable group of wyrdkin and training them in martial combat, he intends to storm Ramsted and take over the city, restoring what he views as the rightful owners to power.

The uprising promises to be brutal and bloody, with Immasi not seeming very keen on accepting anything other than total and unconditional surrender

Ercima-Centira/Setax/Ramsted-Area/Alayna.title: Alayna’s Safehouse
Ercima-Centira/Setax/Ramsted-Area/Alayna.content:
The previously ramshackle house that was some government members hideout. It was taken by The Party as their defacto base of operations after assassinating the previous owner at Nightshade Shadowsbane’s request.
It is in the Old Goverment District of Ramsted.
The party hid Alayna Clorvus in here to keep her safe from anyone who may be interested in finding her
The safehouse location has been told/shown to Rosenbaum Lent.

Ercima-Centira/Setax/Ramsted-Area/Basogr.title: Basogre's Lair
Ercima-Centira/Setax/Ramsted-Area/Basogr.content:
A staging ground for wrestling, feasting on Ill-begotten animals or people, and disgruntled plotting on how to best bash Immasi‘s head to let Basogre eat as many people as he desires.
The lair is also used as a convenient safe house for Puss and his crew.
Located in the lair is a staff that can call Basogre and his fellows into battle when required. This is actually a hidden Rod.
